title: triticum durum subsp. turgidum
intro: Triticum durum subsp. turgidum, also known as durum wheat, is a subspecies of wheat (Triticum durum) that is widely cultivated for its grain, which is used to produce semolina and pasta. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Triticum durum subsp. turgidum has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, improving gut health, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as an herbal supplement in the form of capsules or tea, as well as being used to produce semolina flour that can be added to food and beverages.
